channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The femosphere is the internet’s toxic women-focused answer to the manosphere
item.description: “Men should always do the chasing, women should seek financial contributions from men.” No, this is not dating advice from the 20th century—it’s appearing in online spaces like TikTok and Youtube, part of what researchers are now naming the femosphere.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Google signs deal with AP to deliver up-to-date news through its Gemini AI chatbot
item.description: Google says its artificial intelligence chatbot Gemini will now deliver up-to-date news from The Associated Press in the tech giant’s first such deal with a news publisher.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court Hears Case on Texas Law Aimed at Blocking Kids from Online Pornography
item.description: The US Supreme Court has heard a case regarding a Texas law aimed at blocking kids from seeing online pornography, with justices seeming open to the measure but also raising concerns about its potential impact on adults' free-speech rights.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with explosion of online porn
item.description: The Supreme Court weighed whether an explosion in online pornography requires repudiating the court's precedents concerning sexual content as the justices Wednesday heard arguments in a challenge to Texas’s age-verification law for porn websites.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Chinese cars are close to a US ban – and that could make EVs a lot more expensive
item.description: As part of a crackdown on vehicle software and hardware from China, the Biden Administration has finalized its rules for banning Chinese EVs in the US market.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The new app that is an essential tool for tracking California wildfires
item.description: Frustrated by available information about wildfires, Healdsburg's John Mill built Watch Duty to change that.
item.dc:creator: Lisa M. Krieger, The Mercury News

item.title: Nvidia abandons the problematic 12VHPWR RTX 4080 power connector with a much longer one for the RTX 5080 FE according to leaked images
item.description: Nvidia's made me happy with its DLSS 4 generosity and affordable RTX 5070 price, and that's rolled on with the new and improved RTX 5080 power adapter.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Mac malware mayhem as 100 million Apple users at risk of having personal data stolen
item.description: New Mac malware variant targets browser credentials and personal data, researchers find. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives tips to safeguard your Mac from the latest malware threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Biden signs executive order to open up federal land to speed up AI data center construction
item.description: DoD and DoE set to identify “frontier AI data center” locations with high compute power and clean energy.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The 2025 BAFTA nominees have been revealed, and Conclave is the big winner – but one of my favorite movies got nothing, and it's a crime
item.description: The official 2025 BAFTA nominations have been announced, but I'm not impressed that one of 2024's best movies was completely overlooked.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Why you should use Docker and OCI containers
item.description: A book published in 1981, called Nailing Jelly to a Tree, describes software as “nebulous and difficult to get a firm grip on.” That was true in 1981, and it is no less true four decades later.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: California pulls back EPA requests for approval to phase out diesel-powered trains and big rigs
item.description: California has withdrawn requests for the EPA to give the state authority to enforce rules limiting emissions from diesel-powered trains and big rigs.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Austin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg will cohost reception with Republican billionaires for Trump inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is cohosting a reception with billionaire Republican donors next week for Donald Trump’s inauguration, the latest sign of the Facebook founder's embrace of the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden signs ambitious order to bolster energy resources for AI data centers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden on Tuesday signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations, such as large-scale data centers and new clean power facilities, can be built quickly and at scale in the United
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tennessee Can Enforce Age Verification Law for Pornographic Websites
item.description: A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law requiring pornographic websites to verify visitors' age, despite the First Amendment debate rising to the U.S. Supreme Court over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A possible TikTok ban is just days away. A list of other apps available
item.description: With a possible TikTok ban just days away, many U.S. users are looking for alternative social media platforms to help them keep up with pop culture or provide the type of entertaining videos that popularized the short-form video app.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate Democrat plans legislation to delay TikTok ban
item.description: Sen. Ed Markey (D-Mass.) said Monday he is planning to introduce legislation to give the Chinese parent company of TikTok more time to sell the app as the ban deadline looms less than a week away. “As the Jan.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Trump says he had 'very good experience with TikTok' as court considers ban
item.description: President-elect Trump said Monday he had a “very good experience with TikTok” as he awaits the Supreme Court’s decision on a law that could ban the popular video-sharing platform starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Europe takes a second look at Apple’s DMA compliance
item.description: The European Commission (EC) might have at last seen the sense of pulling back on some of its scrutiny of big tech, which could yet benefit Apple and Google. For many, this will be a welcome step, though critics will continue to criticize.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: For Microsoft, 2025 could be a game-changing year
item.description: It’s looking like 2025 will be one of the most consequential years for Microsoft in a long time. Since Satya Nadella became CEO in early 2014, the company has been on an upward trajectory, despite a few bumps along the way.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The cloud cost wake-up call I predicted
item.description: For years, I’ve cautioned organizations about the hidden downsides of cloud computing. Ironically, I’m “Dave the cloud guy” who warns enterprises about the cloud. The benefits of the cloud’s agility, scalability, and innovation can quickly become a financial sinkhole without proper management.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations. The move will impact students at elementary and high schools across the South American nation starting in February.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: CISA aims to help cyber, tech pros find their path
item.description: CISA’s National Initiative for Cybersecurity Careers and Studies helps cyber pros keep up with technology changes ranging from AI to zero trust. The post CISA aims to help cyber, tech pros find their path first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Five takeaways from GSA’s latest Section 508 assessment
item.description: Agencies are dedicating more time and staff to digital accessibility efforts, but acquisition is still a key hurdle when it comes to Section 508 compliance. The post Five takeaways from GSA’s latest Section 508 assessment first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: The top five procurement themes for 2025
item.description: 2025 will see continued focus on supply chain resiliency, often revolving around support for and investment in the domestic industrial base. The post The top five procurement themes for 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia year in review
item.description: 2024 was Nvidia’s year. Its command of mindshare and market share was unequaled among tech vendors. Here’s a recap of some of the key events of 2024 that highlight just how powerful the world’s most dominant chip player is.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: This fake app clone will steal everything you type on your Android
item.description: Security researchers have found a fake app pretending to be a version of the messaging app Telegram, and hackers are using the app to spread malware.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to use the new Lock object in C# 13
item.description: The C# programming language has provided support for thread synchronization using the lock keyword since its earliest versions. By using a lock statement, you ensure that only one thread can execute the body of the statement at a time. Any other thread is blocked until the lock is released.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation
